revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:旧造幣寮鋳造所正面玄関]]
'''Former Mint Bureau Coin Production Hall Front Entrance''' (旧造幣寮鋳造所正面玄関, Kyū Zōheiryō Chūzōsho Shōmen Genkan) is a preserved architectural structure that served as the front entrance of the minting facility for gold and silver coins of the Mint Bureau (now the {{ill|Japan Mint|en|Japan Mint|qid=Q1130777|lt=Japan Mint|1=Japan Mint}}). It is located along the {{ill|Kyū-Yodo River|en|Kyū-Yodo River|qid=Q5367937|lt=Okawa River (Osaka)|1=Kyū-Yodo River}}, in {{ill|Kita-ku, Osaka|en|Kita-ku, Osaka|qid=Q1207746|lt=Kita-ku, Osaka|1=Kita-ku, Osaka}}.
Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}, it is one of the oldest examples of modern architecture in Japan, alongside the adjacent {{ill|Senpukan|en|Senpukan|qid=Q7450922|lt=Senpukan|1=Senpukan}}.
== History ==
* {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): Completed as the Mint Bureau under the design of {{ill|Thomas Waters|en|Thomas Waters|qid=Q7794915|lt=Thomas Waters|1=Thomas Waters}}. * {{ill|1927|en|1927|qid=Q18110|lt=1927|1=1927}} (Shōwa 2): Demolished due to aging, but the stone materials of the entrance were preserved. * {{ill|1935|en|1935|qid=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10): The preserved stones were used to reconstruct the entrance for the '''Meiji Emperor Memorial Hall''' (later renamed Seitokukan) in its original form. * Post-war: Became the '''{{ill|Sakuramiya Public Hall|ja|桜宮公会堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramiya Public Hall|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 旧造幣寮鋳造所正面玄関|1=Former Coinage Bureau Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511137}}'''. * {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}}: The second floor was used by the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Library|ja|大阪市立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Municipal Library|1=Osaka Municipal Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11440401}}. * {{ill|1956|en|1956|qid=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31): Designated as a National Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|qid=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}}: The library was renamed the Sakuramiya Library. * {{ill|1980|en|1980|qid=Q2439|lt=1980|1=1980}}: Sakuramiya Library was closed. * {{ill|1984|en|1984|qid=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}} (Shōwa 59): Converted by the {{ill|Osaka City Board of Education|ja|大阪市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Board of Education|1=Osaka City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11440269}} into the '''Youth Art Gallery''', used for exhibitions by schoolchildren. * {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}} (Heisei 19): The Youth Art Gallery was closed. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|qid=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}: Reopened as a publicly accessible facility and restaurant under a private revitalization initiative.<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/hodoshiryo/yutoritomidori/0000139318.html Osaka City Press Release: Revitalization and utilization of the Important Cultural Property "Senpukan"], February 18, 2010.</ref><ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/hodoshiryo/yutoritomidori/0000155803.html Osaka City Press Release: Change in utilization project for the "Senpukan Area"], February 10, 2012.</ref>
== Building Features ==
* The entrance features six {{ill|Tuscan order columns|ja|トスカナ式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tuscan order columns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Classical order|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q217175}} made of Seiryozan stone, supporting a triangular gable roof ({{ill|Pediment|en|Pediment|qid=Q190996|lt=Pediment|1=Pediment}}). It measures approximately 2.85 meters in width and over 15 meters in depth. * The roof is a single-layer gable design with copper plating. * The main door is flanked by circular arched windows. * When it was the Meiji Emperor Memorial Hall, the first floor served as an auditorium, while the second floor housed an exhibition room. During its use as the Youth Art Gallery, the first floor was also converted into an exhibition space. * Selected as one of Japan's 50 Modern Heritage Sites.<ref name="旧造幣寮鋳造所">[http://www.adnet.jp/nikkei/kindai/50/ Former Mint Bureau Coin Production Hall]</ref> * Recognized with the 2014 Good Design Award as the former Sakuramiya Public Hall.<ref>{{Good Design Award|41641|Awarded for "Former Sakuramiya Public Hall"}}</ref>
== Location and Access ==
* Location: 1-chome Tenmabashi, Kita-ku, Osaka * Access: ** Immediately west of {{ill|Sakuramiya Bridge|ja|桜宮橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramiya Bridge|1=Sakuranomiya Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11248729}} (Ginbashi) on {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|qid=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 1}} (Sonezaki Street). ** Nearby stations: ** {{ill|Ōsakajō-kitazume Station|en|Ōsakajō-kitazume Station|qid=Q585132|lt=Osaka Castle Kitazume Station|1=Ōsakajō-kitazume Station}} (JR Tozai Line) ** {{ill|Ōsakatemmangū Station|en|Ōsakatemmangū Station|qid=Q1073324|lt=Osaka Tenmangu Station|1=Ōsakatemmangū Station}} (JR Tozai Line)
== References ==
* "Architecture and Society," Vol. 18, Issue 21 (Published by the Japan Architectural Association, December 1935). * "Cultural Properties of Osaka City" (Supervised by the Osaka City Board of Education, published by the Osaka City Cultural Properties Association, 1997).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.oml.city.osaka.lg.jp//?page_id=424 History of the Osaka Municipal Library: Wartime and Post-War Reconstruction Period] Osaka Municipal Library
{{Osaka Urban Landscape Architecture Award 30–34}}{{Good Design Award Architectural Division}}
